message:
fix(mux/domain): [HIGH] offload synchronous spawn_command from async executor (ft-s9oci)

LocalDomain::spawn_pane is an async fn, but the inner
`pair.slave.spawn_command(cmd)` call is fully synchronous on every
backend: fork+exec on Unix, CreateProcess on Windows. Any slow child
load (NSS lookup, LD_PRELOAD ctor deadlock, slow page-in on the child
binary, interposed fork hook) blocks the executor thread for as long
as the child takes to come up. The async runtime loses a worker; every
other async task multiplexed on it (IPC handlers, tmux event
processing, UI repaint) stalls until the spawn settles.

## Fix

Destructure `pair` into its `slave` and `master` components, move the
slave + CommandBuilder into `promise::spawn::spawn_into_new_thread`
(which runs the closure on a dedicated OS thread and returns an
awaitable Task), and await the result on the async side. The executor
thread now yields back to its scheduler for the duration of the fork.

Why `promise::spawn::spawn_into_new_thread` and not a feature-gated
asupersync call: mux is a wezterm-derived crate that doesn't pull
asupersync in directly. `promise` is already a transitive dep, already
wired through the `async-asupersync` / `async-smol` feature fan-out,
and its `spawn_into_new_thread` gives us exactly the "one-shot OS
thread, awaitable result" primitive without widening mux's Cargo.toml.
Same pattern is used by `frankenterm/pty/examples/whoami_async.rs` but
that example reaches for asupersync directly because it's an
example, not a library crate.

## Silent-write follow-up (item 2) — no change needed

The bead also called out `write!(writer, "{err:#}").ok()` on the
child-spawn-failure branch silently swallowing its own write error.
That was already fixed in f6019f0f ("log(mux/domain): expose silenced
error path"): the `.ok()` was replaced with an explicit
`and_then(flush)` check that emits `tracing::warn!` with both the
original spawn_error and the secondary display_result. Verified the
current code at domain.rs:740-749 already carries that fix.

## Verification

`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p1-orch-target cargo check -p mux --lib`
→ Finished clean (0 warnings, 0 errors).

Full `cargo test -p mux` was not run because the mux test suite
requires a display / tty harness that isn't available in this
environment; the change is structural (move a sync call into a
worker thread) and the shape of child_result is preserved, so the
existing Ok/Err branches continue to work unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
